     The Numbers Business Report

Catch That Kid (2004)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $16,703,799Details
International Box Office $255,815
Worldwide Box Office $16,959,614
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Athletic 12-year-old Maddy shares an enthusiasm for mountain climbing with her father, Tom. Unfortunately, Tom suffers a spinal injury while scaling Mount Everest, and his family is unable to afford the surgery that can save him. Maddy decides to get the money for her father’s operation by robbing a high-security bank. She relies on her climbing skills and help from her geeky friends to pull it off successfully.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$5,824,860 (34.9% of total gross)
Legs:2.87 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:98.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$18,000,000 (worldwide box office is 0.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,847 opening theaters/2,848 max. theaters, 3.1 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $32,170,283

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: February 6th, 2004 (Wide) by 20th Century Fox
International Releases: April 8th, 2004 (Limited) (Australia)
Video Release: June 1st, 2004 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG
Running Time: 91 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Foreign-Language Remake, Remake
Plot point: Heist, Kid Spies, Medical and Hospitals, Money Troubles
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production Countries: United States, Germany
Languages: English

Synopsis

Athletic 12-year-old Maddy shares an enthusiasm for mountain climbing with her father, Tom. Unfortunately, Tom suffers a spinal injury while scaling Mount Everest, and his family is unable to afford the surgery that can save him. Maddy decides to get the money for her father’s operation by robbing a high-security bank. She relies on her climbing skills and help from her geeky friends to pull it off successfully.
